meta.title: Categorizing societies
meta.pdfFilename: 6. P1 - Categorizing societies 社会分类.pdf
meta.legacyPath: 睡着过项目组/2. 所有文章(11.20)[192篇]/6. P1 - Categorizing societies 社会分类/
meta.legacyFilename: 6. P1 - Categorizing societies 社会分类html.html

children.text: You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1–13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

When research scholars, including archaeologists and anthropologists, study societies past or present, it can be very useful to have a system of ranking against which to test their ideas. A four-fold categorizing system was developed by the American anthropologist Elman Service; each grouping is associated with certain types of site and settlement. The four types are known as bands, tribes, chiefdoms and early states.

These are small-scale societies, generally of fewer than 100 people, who live by hunting and gathering, moving seasonally to take advantage of wild (undomesticated) food sources. Most groups who live in this way today, such as the Hadza of Tanzania or the San of southern Africa, would be classified as bands. The members of one band are generally related to each other, either by descent or by marriage. Bands lack formal leaders, so there are no clear economic differences or other differences in status among the members. Because bands are composed of mobile groups, their sites consist mainly of seasonally occupied camps and some other sites, such as work sites, where tools are made or other specific activities are carried out. Archaeological sites associated with this type of society may show evidence of insubstantial dwellings, along with the debris of residential occupation. Most sites from the Palaeolithic period (more than 12,000 years ago) seem to be associated with groups of this type.

These are generally larger than bands but rarely number more than a few thousand people, whose diet is mainly based on plants or domestic animals. Typically, these people are settled farmers, but for some groups life is nomadic, with a mobile economy based on herds of animals. Although some tribes have officials, these lack the economic base necessary for effective use of power. The typical settlement pattern for tribes is one of permanent agricultural homesteads or villages. Characteristically, no one settlement dominates any of the others in the region. Instead, the archaeologist often finds evidence of isolated, permanently occupied houses, or permanent villages. These latter may be made up of a collection of free-standing houses like those of the first farmers of the Danube Valley in Europe, or their houses may be grouped together, as in the pueblos of the American Southwest, or the early farming village of Catalhoyuk in what is now Turkey.

These operate on the principle of ranking—differences in social status between people. Different lineages (groups claiming descent from a common ancestor) are graded on a scale of prestige, and the senior lineage, and hence the society as a whole, is governed by a chief. Prestige and rank are determined by how closely related one is to the chief, and there is no true stratification into classes. The role of the chief is crucial.

Often, there is local specialisation in craft products such as pottery, cloth and leatherware, and any surplus of these and of foodstuffs is periodically paid to the chief. He uses these to pay his retainers and may also redistribute them to his subjects as rewards. The chiefdom generally has a centre of power, often with temples, residences of the chief and his retainers, and craft specialists. Chiefdoms vary greatly in size, but the range is generally between 5,000 and 20,000 persons. Chiefdoms give indications that some sites were more important than others and may have operated as permanent ritual and ceremonial centres, although they were not centres with an established bureaucracy. Examples are Moundville in Alabama, USA, or the late-Neolithic monuments of Wessex in southern Britain, including the famous ceremonial centre of Stonehenge.

These preserve many of the features of chiefdoms, but the ruler, perhaps a king or queen, has explicit authority to establish laws and to enforce them by means of a standing army. The society no longer depends on kin relationships but is stratified into different classes. Agricultural workers and the poorer urban dwellers make up the base of the pyramid, with the craft specialists above them and the priests and relatives of the ruler higher still. The society is regarded as a territory owned by the ruling lineage and populated by tenants who have the obligation to pay taxes. The central capital houses the officials of a bureaucratic administration. One of their main functions is to collect revenue (often in the form of taxes and tolls) and distribute it to government, army and craft specialists. Many early states developed complex redistribution systems to support these essential services.

Early-state societies show a characteristic settlement pattern in which cities play the predominant part. The city is typically a large population centre of more than 5,000 people, with major public buildings, and often there is a pronounced settlement hierarchy with the capital city as the major centre and subsidiary or regional centres as well as local villages.

Certainly, it would be wrong to over-emphasise the importance of the four types of society given above, or to spend too long agonising as to whether a particular society should be classified in one category or another. However, in seeking to talk about early societies, we must use words and hence concepts to do so. Elman Service's categories provide us with a good framework to organise our thoughts. They should not, however, deflect us from focusing on changes over time in the different institutions of a society, whether in the social sphere, the organisation of the food quest, technology, contact and exchange, or the spiritual life.
Do the following statements agree with the information given in Reading Passage 1?
In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet write
if the statement agrees with the information
if the statement contradicts the information
if there is no information on this
There is usually little difference in wealth between the various members of a band.
In tribes, farmers typically grow a wide range of food plants.
A typical tribe has one settlement which is more important than others.
In a chiefdom, social status usually depends on the amount of land a person owns.
A chiefdom typically contains some workers who are engaged in making goods.
An early state may depend on military power to maintain law and order.
Bureaucratic officials in early states receive higher salaries than any other workers.

explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：游群各成员之间的财富差异通常很小。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Bands” 段中 “there are no clear economic differences or other differences in status among the members”，明确说明游群成员间无明显经济差异，即财富差异很小，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：在部落中，农民通常种植多种粮食作物。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：“Tribes” 段仅提到部落 “饮食主要基于植物或家养动物”“多为定居农民”，但未提及农民 “种植的粮食作物种类是否繁多”，既无肯定也无否定信息，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。
题目翻译：典型的部落有一个比其他定居点更重要的定居点。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “no one settlement dominates any of the others in the region”，说明部落中无任何定居点比其他定居点更重要，与题干 “一处定居点更重要” 的描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：在酋长制社会中，社会地位通常取决于个人拥有的土地数量。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “Prestige and rank are determined by how closely related one is to the chief”，说明酋长制社会的地位由与首领的血缘亲近度决定，而非 “拥有土地的数量”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。
题目翻译：酋长制社会通常包含一些从事商品制作的劳动者。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “local specialisation in craft products such as pottery, cloth and leatherware”，说明酋长制社会存在手工业专业化分工，即有从事商品制作的劳动者，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：早期国家可能依赖军事力量维持法律和秩序。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Early states” 段中 “the ruler, perhaps a king or queen, has explicit authority to establish laws and to enforce them by means of a standing army”，说明早期国家的统治者通过常备军执法，即依赖军事力量维持秩序，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。
题目翻译：早期国家的官僚官员薪资高于其他任何劳动者。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：“Early states” 段仅提到官僚机构 “收税并分配给政府、军队和手工业者”，但未提及官员 “工资是否高于其他劳动者”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

items.text:
（1）题目 1：There is usually little difference in wealth between the various members of a band.
题目翻译：游群各成员之间的财富差异通常很小。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Bands” 段中 “there are no clear economic differences or other differences in status among the members”，明确说明游群成员间无明显经济差异，即财富差异很小，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（2）题目 2：In tribes, farmers typically grow a wide range of food plants.
题目翻译：在部落中，农民通常种植多种粮食作物。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：“Tribes” 段仅提到部落 “饮食主要基于植物或家养动物”“多为定居农民”，但未提及农民 “种植的粮食作物种类是否繁多”，既无肯定也无否定信息，信息缺失，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

（3）题目 3：A typical tribe has one settlement which is more important than others.
题目翻译：典型的部落有一个比其他定居点更重要的定居点。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “no one settlement dominates any of the others in the region”，说明部落中无任何定居点比其他定居点更重要，与题干 “一处定居点更重要” 的描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（4）题目 4：In a chiefdom, social status usually depends on the amount of land a person owns.
题目翻译：在酋长制社会中，社会地位通常取决于个人拥有的土地数量。
答案：FALSE（错误）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “Prestige and rank are determined by how closely related one is to the chief”，说明酋长制社会的地位由与首领的血缘亲近度决定，而非 “拥有土地的数量”，与题干描述矛盾，因此判定为 FALSE。

（5）题目 5：A chiefdom typically contains some workers who are engaged in making goods.
题目翻译：酋长制社会通常包含一些从事商品制作的劳动者。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “local specialisation in craft products such as pottery, cloth and leatherware”，说明酋长制社会存在手工业专业化分工，即有从事商品制作的劳动者，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（6）题目 6：An early state may depend on military power to maintain law and order.
题目翻译：早期国家可能依赖军事力量维持法律和秩序。
答案：TRUE（正确）
解析：定位 “Early states” 段中 “the ruler, perhaps a king or queen, has explicit authority to establish laws and to enforce them by means of a standing army”，说明早期国家的统治者通过常备军执法，即依赖军事力量维持秩序，与题干描述一致，因此判定为 TRUE。

（7）题目 7：Bureaucratic officials in early states receive higher salaries than any other workers.
题目翻译：早期国家的官僚官员薪资高于其他任何劳动者。
答案：NOT GIVEN（未提及）
解析：“Early states” 段仅提到官僚机构 “收税并分配给政府、军队和手工业者”，但未提及官员 “工资是否高于其他劳动者”，无相关信息，因此判定为 NOT GIVEN。

children.text: NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND / OR A NUMBER
from the passage for each answer.
What items do bands produce at work sites?
Which way of life, apart from settled farming, may be followed by people in tribes?
How were houses arranged in the village of Catalhoyuk?
Which items, apart from craft goods, may be given by a chief to members of his chiefdom?
What is usually the maximum number of people living in a society which has a chief?
Apart from less wealthy inhabitants of cities, which group forms the lowest class in an early state?

explanationSection.sectionTitle: 2. 简答题（Questions 8–13：NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER）
explanationSection.text:
题目翻译：游群在工作地点生产什么物品？
答案：tools（工具）
解析：定位 “Bands” 段中 “work sites, where tools are made or other specific activities are carried out”，明确指出游群在工作地点制作 “工具（tools）”，直接对应题干 “生产的物品”，因此答案为 “tools”。
题目翻译：除定居农耕外，部落居民可能遵循哪种生活方式？
答案：nomadic（游牧）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “for some groups life is nomadic, with a mobile economy based on herds of animals”，说明部落除定居农耕外，部分群体过 “游牧（nomadic）” 生活，符合题干要求，因此答案为 “nomadic”。
题目翻译：恰塔霍裕克村的房屋是如何布局的？
答案：grouped together（集中排列）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “their houses may be grouped together, as in the pueblos of the American Southwest, or the early farming village of Catalhoyuk”，明确提到恰塔霍裕克村的房屋 “集中排列（grouped together）”，因此答案为 “grouped together”。
题目翻译：除手工业品外，首领可能会给酋长制社会成员分发什么物品？
答案：foodstuffs（食物）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “any surplus of these \[craft products\] and of foodstuffs is periodically paid to the chief. He uses these to... redistribute them to his subjects as rewards”，说明首领除了分配手工业品，还会分配 “食物（foodstuffs）”，符合题干 “除手工业品外的物品”，因此答案为 “foodstuffs”。
题目翻译：拥有首领的社会（酋长制社会）通常的最大人口数量是多少？
答案：20,000（20000 人）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “Chiefdoms vary greatly in size, but the range is generally between 5,000 and 20,000 persons”，明确酋长制社会的人口范围为 5000-20000 人，最大人口数量为 “20,000 人”，对应题干 “最大人数”，因此答案为 “20,000”。
题目翻译：除城市贫困居民外，哪一群体构成早期国家的最底层阶级？
答案：agricultural workers（农业劳动者）
解析：定位 “Early states” 段中 “Agricultural workers and the poorer urban dwellers make up the base of the pyramid”，说明早期国家的底层阶级包括 “农业劳动者（agricultural workers）” 和贫困城市居民，符合题干 “除城市贫困居民外的底层群体”，因此答案为 “agricultural workers”。

items.text:
（1）题目 8：What items do bands produce at work sites?
题目翻译：游群在工作地点生产什么物品？
答案：tools（工具）
解析：定位 “Bands” 段中 “work sites, where tools are made or other specific activities are carried out”，明确指出游群在工作地点制作 “工具（tools）”，直接对应题干 “生产的物品”，因此答案为 “tools”。

（2）题目 9：Which way of life, apart from settled farming, may be followed by people in tribes?
题目翻译：除定居农耕外，部落居民可能遵循哪种生活方式？
答案：nomadic（游牧）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “for some groups life is nomadic, with a mobile economy based on herds of animals”，说明部落除定居农耕外，部分群体过 “游牧（nomadic）” 生活，符合题干要求，因此答案为 “nomadic”。

（3）题目 10：How were houses arranged in the village of Catalhoyuk?
题目翻译：恰塔霍裕克村的房屋是如何布局的？
答案：grouped together（集中排列）
解析：定位 “Tribes” 段中 “their houses may be grouped together, as in the pueblos of the American Southwest, or the early farming village of Catalhoyuk”，明确提到恰塔霍裕克村的房屋 “集中排列（grouped together）”，因此答案为 “grouped together”。

（4）题目 11：Which items, apart from craft goods, may be given by a chief to members of his chiefdom?
题目翻译：除手工业品外，首领可能会给酋长制社会成员分发什么物品？
答案：foodstuffs（食物）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “any surplus of these \[craft products\] and of foodstuffs is periodically paid to the chief. He uses these to... redistribute them to his subjects as rewards”，说明首领除了分配手工业品，还会分配 “食物（foodstuffs）”，符合题干 “除手工业品外的物品”，因此答案为 “foodstuffs”。

（5）题目 12：What is usually the maximum number of people living in a society which has a chief?
题目翻译：拥有首领的社会（酋长制社会）通常的最大人口数量是多少？
答案：20,000（20000 人）
解析：定位 “Chiefdoms” 段中 “Chiefdoms vary greatly in size, but the range is generally between 5,000 and 20,000 persons”，明确酋长制社会的人口范围为 5000-20000 人，最大人口数量为 “20,000 人”，对应题干 “最大人数”，因此答案为 “20,000”。

（6）题目 13：Apart from less wealthy inhabitants of cities, which group forms the lowest class in an early state?
题目翻译：除城市贫困居民外，哪一群体构成早期国家的最底层阶级？
答案：agricultural workers（农业劳动者）
解析：定位 “Early states” 段中 “Agricultural workers and the poorer urban dwellers make up the base of the pyramid”，说明早期国家的底层阶级包括 “农业劳动者（agricultural workers）” 和贫困城市居民，符合题干 “除城市贫困居民外的底层群体”，因此答案为 “agricultural workers”。
